name: Tonga
avoid: November–April is hotter, wetter, and overlaps South Pacific cyclone season, so inter-island ferries and small-plane schedules are less reliable.
July–October is peak humpback season in Vava'u, so whale-swim boats, guesthouses, and domestic flights can sell out well in advance.
Sunday is quieter across Tonga: many shops close, public transport is limited, and some family-run eateries reduce hours.
stopoverNote: For trips under 6 days, keep the plan to Tongatapu plus either Vava'u or Ha'apai; trying to cover all three island groups gets transfer-heavy.

cities.name: Nuku'alofa
cities.notes: Tongatapu's capital is the practical arrival base for the Royal Palace waterfront, Talamahu Market, and easy self-drive day trips to Ha'amonga, Anahulu Cave, and the south-coast blowholes.

cities.name: Vava'u
cities.notes: Vava'u centers on Neiafu's protected harbour, humpback-whale season from July to October, and boat days to Swallows Cave, Mariner's Cave, and reef islands such as Nuku.

cities.name: Ha'apai
cities.notes: Ha'apai is the slower barefoot-islands leg built around Pangai logistics, Foa's long beaches, simple reef snorkels, and guesthouse-led boat trips.

itinerary.sim: Digicel Tonga prepaid is the easiest setup in Nuku'alofa; buy it on Tongatapu and download ferry confirmations plus offline maps before heading to Vava'u or Ha'apai.

Nuku'alofa.name: Nuku'alofa
Nuku'alofa.note: Base yourself in Nuku'alofa for one town day and one full Tongatapu loop; the best sights are spread out enough that a rental car or driver saves time.

days.theme: Royal Waterfront, Market Browsing and a Gentle Capital Arrival

activities.name: Royal Palace waterfront viewing
activities.tip: The palace is admired from outside only; go in softer morning or late-afternoon light because interior visits are not generally open to the public.

activities.name: Talamahu Market produce and handicraft stop
activities.tip: Entry is free; budget a few pa'anga for fruit, coconut bread, or woven souvenirs.

activities.name: Friends Cafe coffee and town-center lunch break
Sunset walk along Vuna Road and the Nuku'alofa waterfront

days.theme: Ha'amonga, Anahulu Cave and the South-Coast Blowholes

activities.name: Ha'amonga 'a Maui Trilithon visit
activities.tip: The ancient stone trilithon is usually ungated; arrive early because there is little shade and only occasional caretaker donations.

activities.name: Anahulu Cave freshwater swim
activities.tip: Adult entry is commonly TOP20 cash; bring reef shoes because the cave steps and limestone edges stay slippery.

activities.name: Mapu'a 'a Vaea blowholes stop
activities.tip: The spray is strongest on a rougher tide, and staying back from the edge matters because the rock shelf gets slick.

activities.name: Sunshine Rentals self-drive day on Tongatapu
activities.tip: Budget another ₹1,440–₹2,160 once per trip for the local temporary driving permit that rental desks help arrange.

Vava'u.name: Vava'u
Vava'u.note: Vava'u is worth three slower days if you can spare them: one harbour day, one whale or boat day, and one reef-or-cave outing keeps the pace right.

days.theme: Flight North, Neiafu Harbour and Mt Talau Views

activities.name: Lulutai Airlines flight from Tongatapu to Vava'u
activities.tip: One-way fares usually sit around TOP350–470 depending on baggage and season, and July–October seats should be booked as early as possible.

activities.name: Mount Talau National Park lookout walk
Neiafu harbourfront stroll and market stop

days.theme: Humpback Whale Day or a Relaxed Harbour Backup

activities.name: Vava'u Whale Watching Adventures humpback whale swim day trip (Jul–Oct)
activities.tip: Licensed full-day trips commonly land around TOP500–600 with lunch; if you're outside whale season, swap this for a Nuku Island snorkel day.

activities.name: Bounty Bar & Restaurant sunset dinner

days.theme: Swallows Cave, Mariner's Cave and Vava'u Blue Water

activities.name: Local boat charter to Swallows Cave and Mariner's Cave
activities.tip: Most small-group boat days price around TOP200–300 depending on fuel, cave count, and snorkel inclusions; seas are usually calmer in the morning.

activities.name: Nuku Island beach stop

Ha'apai.name: Ha'apai
Ha'apai.note: Ha'apai works best as a simple beach-and-boat decompression block, so keep expectations low-key and let your lodge shape the day around weather and ferry timing.

days.theme: Inter-Island Arrival, Foa Beaches and a Slow Sunset

activities.name: Friendly Islands Shipping Agency ferry from Vava'u to Ha'apai
activities.tip: Adult deck fares usually sit around TOP100–150 when sailings line up, but schedules are irregular enough that you should reconfirm locally before leaving Vava'u.

activities.name: Foa Island beach time near Sandy Beach Resort or Matafonua Lodge
Resort snorkel gear or kayak rental

days.theme: Uoleva Day Trip, Pangai Errands and a Barefoot Island Rhythm

activities.name: Boat trip to Uoleva Island beaches and reef snorkel
activities.tip: Expect roughly TOP80–120 for a simple guesthouse-arranged boat depending on group size and whether lunch is packed.

activities.name: Pangai town and harbour wander
Compact rental car or island transfer day
activities.tip: Local hires on Ha'apai are limited, so many travelers rely on their lodge to pre-arrange a small car or driver.

connections.method: Lulutai Airlines domestic flight from Fua'amotu to Lupepau'u Airport.
Friendly Islands Shipping Agency inter-island ferry when the weekly sailing aligns; some weeks require backtracking via Tongatapu instead.
Lulutai Airlines domestic flight back to Tongatapu, with ferry as the slower fallback if flights are full or weather-disrupted.

extras: Carry cash outside Tongatapu because fuel surcharges, ferry tickets, cave entry, and family-run lodges often prefer pa'anga over cards.
Book whale-season flights and rooms before you lock the rest of the route; Vava'u fills first and then ferry timing shapes the rest of the itinerary.
A reef-safe rash guard, dry bag, and basic motion-sickness tablets help because small-boat transfers and cave stops are central to a Tonga trip.
If Sunday falls in the middle of your island hops, stock up on snacks the previous evening because shops and cafés may open only briefly.
